title: Bangladesh: World Bank Provides $1.1 Billion Emergency Support to Safeguard Food Security, Livelihoods
summary_ja: 世界銀行は、バングラデシュの食料安全保障と生活を保護するため、11億ドルの緊急支援を承認しました。この支援は、肥料や燃料市場の価格変動と供給不足の影響を緩和し、特に小規模農家への影響を軽減することを目的としています。プロジェクトは、米生産に必要な肥料の輸入を確保し、影響を受けた世帯への現金給付や生計支援を提供します。
summary_en: The World Bank has approved $1.1 billion in emergency support for Bangladesh to safeguard food security and livelihoods. This support aims to mitigate the impact of price and supply volatility in fertilizer and fuel markets, with a particular focus on smallholder farmers. The projects will ensure fertilizer imports critical for rice production and provide cash transfers and livelihood assistance to affected households.

bottlenecks.description: 肥料価格と供給の変動が、バングラデシュの経済、特に小規模農家や貧困層に深刻な影響を与えています。バングラデシュは肥料の85%以上を輸入に依存しています。
bottlenecks.evidence_quote: “Rising food, fertilizer, and fuel prices stemming from the Middle East Conflict, along with tighter fiscal space have deeply impacted Bangladesh’s economy, hitting smallholder farmers as well as poor and vulnerable people the hardest,”

bottlenecks.description: 肥料供給の混乱は、国の食料安全保障を脅かすだけでなく、貧困を深化させ、雇用を失う可能性があります。
bottlenecks.evidence_quote: “Bangladesh’s food security depends on Aman and Boro rice seasons, which together account for about 90% of the country’s total rice production. Further, about half the population is employed in agriculture sector. So, any disruption in fertilizer supply would not only threaten food security, it would deepen poverty and cost jobs,”

success_factors.description: 世界銀行は、バングラデシュの食料安全保障と生活を守るために、肥料供給を確保し、影響を受けた人々や事業への支援を提供することで、緊急支援を拡大しました。
success_factors.evidence_quote: “The World Bank has stepped up with immediate support to help Bangladesh mitigate this impact to ensure fertilizer supply for rice production, protect households, jobs and livelihoods and continue with essential services.”

success_factors.description: 2つのプロジェクトを通じて、肥料輸入、現金給付、生計支援、および必須サービスの継続を支援し、ショックの影響から人々、事業、雇用を保護するための迅速な対応を目指しています。
success_factors.evidence_quote: The Emergency Support for Food Security Project ($300 million) will provide time-bound financing to help Bangladesh import fertilizers critical for the Aman and Boro seasons... The Contingent Emergency Response Project ($713 million) will support quick-disbursing emergency expenditures, including cash transfers and livelihood assistance for affected households and micro, small, and medium enterprises, helping stabilize incomes and preserve jobs during crises. It will also finance fuel and energy supplies to continue essential services...

title: WFP Madagascar Country Brief June 2026
summary_ja: マダガスカルは構造的な脆弱性と干ばつ、サイクロンといった気候変動の影響に苦しむ低所得国です。農業依存度の高さにもかかわらず、市場へのアクセス制限と低生産性が貧困と気候・経済的ショックへの脆弱性を増幅させています。WFPは、人道的支援、気候適応、開発投資を統合し、小規模農家へのレジリエンス支援を含むアプローチを推進しています。
summary_en: Madagascar is a low-income country grappling with structural vulnerabilities and recurrent climate shocks like droughts and cyclones. Despite high dependence on agriculture, limited market access and low productivity perpetuate poverty and heighten exposure to climatic and economic shocks. WFP promotes an integrated approach linking humanitarian action, climate adaptation, and development investments, including resilience support for smallholder households.

bottlenecks.description: 農業従事者の市場へのアクセスが限られていることが、貧困から抜け出せない一因となっています。
bottlenecks.evidence_quote: With more than three quarters of households dependent on agriculture, limited market access and low productivity continue to trap families in poverty and heighten their exposure to climatic and economic shocks.

bottlenecks.description: マダガスカルは構造的な脆弱性に直面しており、それが気候変動の影響を悪化させています。
bottlenecks.evidence_quote: Madagascar remains a low-income country grappling with structural vulnerabilities and recurrent climate shocks, including droughts and cyclones.

bottlenecks.description: 干ばつやサイクロンといった気候変動によるショックが頻繁に発生し、食料不安を悪化させています。
bottlenecks.evidence_quote: Madagascar remains a low-income country grappling with structural vulnerabilities and recurrent climate shocks, including droughts and cyclones.

success_factors.description: 人道的支援、気候適応、開発投資を統合したアプローチが推進されています。
success_factors.evidence_quote: WFP continues to promote an integrated approach that links humanitarian action, climate adaptation, and development investments.

success_factors.description: 小規模農家へのレジリエンス支援が実施されています。
success_factors.evidence_quote: This includes school meals for vulnerable children and resilience support for smallholder households, underpinned by strengthened emergency preparedness and aligned with the WFP Madagascar Country Strategic Plan.

title: Lions Clubs International Foundation and World Food Programme scale up locally sourced school meals for children in Nepal
summary_ja: ネパールで、Lions Clubs International Foundation (LCIF)とWorld Food Programme (WFP)は、地元政府と連携し、地元農家から調達した食材を使用した学校給食プログラムを拡大しています。この取り組みは、学童の栄養改善、出席率向上、そして小規模農家にとっての安定した市場創出を目指しています。
summary_en: In Nepal, the Lions Clubs International Foundation (LCIF) and World Food Programme (WFP), in collaboration with local governments, are scaling up school meal programs that utilize locally sourced ingredients from smallholder farmers. This initiative aims to improve child nutrition and school attendance, while creating stable markets for local farmers.

success_factors.description: 地域農家から調達された食材を使った学校給食は、地元の農家のために安定した市場を創出します。
success_factors.evidence_quote: “Mid-day meals play a vital role in improving attendance and learning outcomes and the Home-grown school feeding approach helps ensure these meals are nutritious and locally sourced, creating stable markets for local farmers,”

success_factors.description: このパートナーシップは、ネパール政府の昼食プログラムを直接支援し、社会保障と地域農業を結びつけることの重要性を強調しています。
success_factors.evidence_quote: As Nepal continues its transition towards a nationally led school meals system, partnership between the Government of Nepal, WFP and LCIF highlights the importance of linking social protection with local agriculture to build stronger, more resilient food systems.

success_factors.description: 1年間のパイロットフェーズを経て、このパートナーシップは次の1年間に拡大されました。
success_factors.evidence_quote: The collaboration follows a one-year pilot phase completed in 2025, during which LCIF and World Food Program USA jointly invested USD 1 million to support school meals programmes in Nepal. Following this success, the partnership has been expanded for the next one year (2026-2027) with a budget of USD 500k jointly invested by LCIF and WFP USA.

title: Nepal Context Report - June 26, 2026
summary_ja: ネパールは地形や気候の多様性により地域間で市場アクセスに格差があり、小規模農家は雨水依存の農業や出稼ぎに大きく依存している。地理的制約、インフラ不足、気候変動による災害が農家の商業的発展や食料安全保障を阻害する構造的な障壁となっている。
summary_en: Nepal's agroecological diversity creates significant regional disparities in market access, with smallholders largely reliant on rainfed agriculture and remittances. Structural constraints, including geographic isolation, limited infrastructure, and recurrent climatic shocks, impede the commercialization of smallholder agriculture and threaten food security.

bottlenecks.description: 地形的要因やインフラの未整備により、市場へのアクセスが限定的である。
bottlenecks.evidence_quote: The Hills and Mountains face structural production deficits due to limited arable land, lower productivity, and high transportation costs.

bottlenecks.description: 小規模で断片化された農地が、食料不安のリスクを高めている。
bottlenecks.evidence_quote: High levels of poverty, small and fragmented landholdings, and uneven access to markets, infrastructure, and services contribute to persistent risk of acute food insecurity

bottlenecks.description: 洪水や地滑りなどの自然災害が農業生産を阻害し、市場機能を一時的に麻痺させている。
bottlenecks.evidence_quote: Recurrent shocks — including floods, landslides, droughts, and economic disruptions — further constrain livelihoods, disrupt agricultural production, and limit market functioning.

title: World: Open letter to address hunger, food insecurity and malnutrition
summary_ja: 本記事は、紛争、経済不安定、気候変動が世界の食料システムに与える影響について、宗教系NGO団体が連名で発表した公開書簡です。脆弱な地域における食料供給網の保護や、小規模農家および気候変動に適応した農業への支援を政府や国際機関に強く求めています。
summary_en: This open letter, issued by global faith-based organizations, addresses the global hunger crisis driven by conflict, economic instability, and climate shocks. It calls on governments and international institutions to prioritize humanitarian access, strengthen resilient food systems, and provide direct support to smallholder farmers and climate-resilient agriculture.

bottlenecks.description: 紛争や混乱による食料供給網、市場、インフラの破壊が、食料価格の上昇と入手困難を招いています。
bottlenecks.evidence_quote: The destruction of livelihoods, agricultural systems, markets and infrastructure is devastating communities directly affected by violence. At the same time, disruptions to energy supplies, fertiliser markets, shipping routes and humanitarian access are sending shockwaves through global food systems

success_factors.description: 政府や国際機関が、小規模農家や気候適応型農業を支援する政策を優先的に実施することが成功の鍵として示唆されています。
success_factors.evidence_quote: We therefore call on governments, multilateral institutions, donors and faith communities to: ... Support smallholder farmers, local food producers and climate-resilient agriculture, especially in fragile and conflict-affected contexts.

title: Benin Context Report, June 11, 2026
summary_ja: ベナンは雨水依存の小規模農家が主体であり、南部では市場統合が進んでいる一方、北部は紛争や異常気象の影響を受けています。食料安全保障上の懸念が高まる中、農家は価格高騰や市場アクセスの制限といった構造的な課題に直面しています。
summary_en: Benin's agriculture is dominated by rainfed smallholders, with relatively strong market integration in the south but significant challenges in the north. The sector faces structural vulnerabilities, including conflict, erratic rainfall, and rising input costs, which hinder stability and food security.

bottlenecks.description: 北部では治安悪化により移動が制限され、市場へのアクセスが困難になっています。
bottlenecks.evidence_quote: Growing insecurity is disrupting livelihoods, restricting mobility, limiting access to agricultural land and markets, and driving forced, often repeated displacements.

bottlenecks.description: 農業資材の価格上昇が農家の購買力を低下させています。
bottlenecks.evidence_quote: Rising food, fuel, and agricultural input prices, especially in conflict-affected areas, are also eroding purchasing power for poor households.

bottlenecks.description: 洪水や降雨パターンの不安定さが農業生産の障壁となっています。
bottlenecks.evidence_quote: Additional hazards include recurrent flooding in southern and riverine areas and erratic rainfall and dry spells in the north.

title: WFP and Government of Nepal Launch Innovation Accelerator for Climate and Food Security
summary_ja: ネパールにて国連世界食糧計画（WFP）と政府が、適応基金気候イノベーション・アクセラレーター（AFCIA）の実施フェーズを開始しました。本プログラムは、気候変動リスクへの適応を支援するため、デジタルツールや市場参入・バリューチェーン革新を行う現地スタートアップ4社を支援し、小規模農家の強靭性強化を図ります。
summary_en: WFP and the Government of Nepal have launched the implementation phase of the Adaptation Fund Climate Innovation Accelerator (AFCIA) to strengthen climate resilience for smallholders. The program supports four local startups focusing on digital advisory, insurance, and market and value chain innovations to help farmers adapt to climate risks.

bottlenecks.description: 洪水、干ばつ、熱波などの極端な気象事象が小規模農家の生活や食料システムを脅かしている。
bottlenecks.evidence_quote: The solutions are designed to help communities better adapt to rising climate risks - including floods, droughts, heatwaves and extreme weather - that threaten lives, livelihoods and food systems across the country.

success_factors.description: 政府、開発パートナー、学術界、民間セクターが連携し、気候変動に強いソリューションの拡大を目指している。
success_factors.evidence_quote: The event brought together representatives from government, development partners, academia and the private sector to explore pathways for scaling climate-smart solutions nationwide.

success_factors.description: デジタルアドバイザリープラットフォームや衛星ベースの保険ツール、デジタル化された有機バリューチェーンを活用している。
success_factors.evidence_quote: The accelerator supports local start-ups to pilot and scale solutions across the food system, including solar-powered irrigation and clean energy solutions, digital advisory platforms for farmers, satellite-based insurance and climate data tools and market and value chain innovations for smallholders.

title: Honduras: Key Message Update - Fuel and fertilizer price increases compound delayed primera planting, May - September 2026
summary_ja: ホンジュラスでは燃料・肥料価格の高騰と降雨不足により、小規模農家の生産コストが増大し、作付けの遅延や収量減少が懸念されている。これらの要因が農家の商業的生産活動を強く圧迫しており、食料不安を悪化させている。
summary_en: In Honduras, rising fuel and fertilizer costs combined with below-average rainfall are increasing production costs and delaying planting for smallholders, with expected negative impacts on crop yields. These factors are severely hindering agricultural production and exacerbating food insecurity for small-scale farmers.

bottlenecks.description: 肥料や燃料の価格が高騰し、生産コストを押し上げている。
bottlenecks.evidence_quote: Above-average fuel and fertilizer prices continue to drive high production and transportation costs for a second consecutive month.

bottlenecks.description: 降雨不足により、土壌水分が不足し、作付け開始が遅延している。
bottlenecks.evidence_quote: Recent rainfall estimates through mid-May indicate widespread below-average precipitation across Honduras, negatively impacting primera land preparation and planting in localized areas.

title: AI offers promise for agriculture, but smallholder farmers risk being left behind - The Conversation
summary_ja: AI技術は農業に革新をもたらす可能性があるが、小規模農家はデジタル格差やリソース不足により取り残されるリスクがある。特にコストやインフラの制約が、彼らの技術導入や商業的成功を阻む障壁となっている。
summary_en: AI offers potential for agricultural transformation, yet smallholder farmers face the risk of being left behind due to digital divides and resource constraints. The lack of infrastructure and high costs remain significant barriers to their adoption of these technologies.

bottlenecks.description: デジタルリテラシーの不足や、AI技術を農場へ応用する方法に関する知識の欠如。
bottlenecks.evidence_quote: Many smallholder farmers lack the digital literacy and technical knowledge required to leverage these tools effectively.

bottlenecks.description: 高額なサブスクリプション費用やデバイス購入費が経済的な障壁となっている。
bottlenecks.evidence_quote: High costs of subscription services and hardware can be prohibitive for farmers with limited capital.

bottlenecks.description: 農村部における安定したインターネット接続や電力といった物理的インフラの不足。
bottlenecks.evidence_quote: Poor connectivity and lack of electricity in rural areas hinder the adoption of digital technologies.
